I'll analyze both fixes first, then read the file and apply the changes.

This means the interface was likely just reorganized/moved but keeps the same name and props. The incident context shows `Chart` is imported from `@patternfly/react-charts`. Since the component and props names haven't changed, this is likely a no-op at the usage level - the `Chart` component still exists with the same props.

**Fix 2**: Same pattern - `ChartAreaProps` was removed but migrates to `ChartAreaProps` with identity member mappings. `ChartArea` is imported from `@patternfly/react-charts`. Again, the component still exists with the same name and props.

**Fix 1 Analysis**: The rule says `ChartProps` interface was removed, but the migration target is still `ChartProps` with all identity member mappings. The component usage with its props (`containerComponent`, `domain`, `height`, `padding`, `scale`, `width`) all have identity mappings in the migration, so they remain unchanged.

The file uses `ChartArea` in JSX with `style` and `data` props. Since the component still exists with the same name and the same props, no code change is needed.

Both fixes target interfaces (`ChartProps` and `ChartAreaProps`) that this file doesn't directly reference. The file only imports and uses the components (`Chart` and `ChartArea`), which still exist with the same names and accept the same props. No changes are required.

2. **Fix 2** (line 9, `ChartAreaProps` removed → `ChartAreaProps`): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The file imports `ChartArea` (the component), not `ChartAreaProps` (the interface). The `ChartArea` component still exists with the same name and the same props used in this file (`style`, `data`). All member mappings are identity.
